description: Replace the side panel's indirect round-trip data flow with direct push-based updates. The background script will forward plugin state and tab state changes directly to the side panel, eliminating unnecessary fetchConfigState() round-trips and ensuring the side panel always reflects the latest MCP server state in real-time.

userStories.title: Forward tab.stateChanged to side panel from background
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel tab state dots to update in real-time when I open, close, or navigate tabs so that I always see accurate plugin status without waiting for unrelated events to trigger a refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: After checkTabStateChanges sends tab.stateChanged to the MCP server, it also calls forwardToSidePanel with the same tab.stateChanged data wrapped in sp:serverMessage
The forwardToSidePanel call happens immediately after the sendToServer call in the same code path
The side panel's existing tab.stateChanged handler in App.tsx (lines 83-93) receives these messages and updates the specific plugin's tabState in the plugins array
No new message types are introduced — uses existing sp:serverMessage with tab.stateChanged method

userStories.title: Push sync.full plugin state directly to side panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to update immediately when the MCP server sends a full sync instead of waiting for a round-trip config.getState request, so plugin changes appear instantly.
acceptanceCriteria: handleSyncFull in message-router.ts forwards a new sp:serverMessage with method 'plugins.state' containing the full plugin list (name, displayName, version, trustTier, tabState, urlPatterns, tools with enabled status) directly to the side panel
The plugin data is built from the validated plugin payloads that handleSyncFull already processes, plus tab state from the lastKnownState cache in tab-state.ts
A new exported function in tab-state.ts returns the current lastKnownState for a given plugin name (or 'closed' as default)
App.tsx handles the 'plugins.state' notification by calling setPlugins() directly with the received plugin data, without triggering fetchConfigState()
The synthetic 'plugins.changed' notification in handleSyncFull (lines 289-292) is replaced with the new 'plugins.state' notification
The PluginState type / ConfigStatePlugin type is compatible with the data shape forwarded
fetchConfigState() is still called on initial mount (getConnectionState → loadPlugins) as a fallback, but is no longer triggered by sync.full

userStories.title: Push plugin.update state directly to side panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want hot-reloaded plugin changes to appear in the side panel immediately without a round-trip, matching the pattern established for sync.full.
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginUpdate in message-router.ts forwards a sp:serverMessage with method 'plugin.updated' containing the single updated plugin in ConfigStatePlugin format
App.tsx handles the 'plugin.updated' notification by updating the specific plugin in the plugins array (replace by name), or adding it if not present
The synthetic 'plugins.changed' notification in handlePluginUpdate (lines 322-325) is replaced with the new 'plugin.updated' notification

userStories.title: Remove ws:message broadcast fallback in side panel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the fragile ws:message broadcast fallback with the hardcoded 1.5s delay since the primary push-based data flow is now reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: The ws:message handler in App.tsx (the block around lines 122-134 that listens for sync.full and calls setTimeout with 1500ms) is removed
The comment explaining the fallback is removed
No other code references or depends on this fallback behavior

userStories.title: Optimistic local toggle for tool enable/disable
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool toggles to feel instant by updating the UI immediately when I click, instead of waiting for a server round-trip to refetch the entire plugin state.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard's handleToggleAll and handleToggleTool update local plugins state optimistically before the server request completes
The .then(onRefresh) chain is removed from both toggle handlers — no fetchConfigState() round-trip on toggle
If the server request fails, the optimistic update is reverted (toggle flips back) and the error toast is shown
The onRefresh prop is removed from PluginCard and PluginList since it is no longer needed for toggle operations
The loadPlugins callback and its ref in App.tsx can remain for initial load and the plugins.changed fallback, but are no longer passed through PluginList/PluginCard for toggles
A setPlugins updater function is passed to PluginList/PluginCard instead of onRefresh, or the toggle logic is lifted to App.tsx, or an alternative clean approach is used
